guarantees.title: SHA-256 hash chain · HMAC per entry

loopValidator.heading: The validator the OODA Loop was missing — closed by design.
loopValidator.sub: Borjie ships the quality-gate validator the IEEE Spectrum + Snyk paper says agentic AI is missing. Every action Mr. Mwikila proposes passes a mandatory five-gate check before any persistence, notification, or external write.

paper.title: Agentic AI's OODA Loop Problem
paper.finding: Agents running Observe → Orient → Decide → Act loops at machine speed without an explicit validator between Decide and Act don't compound value — they compound error. The fix is a slow, explicit, validating layer that sits between the decision and the action.
paper.quote: Fast cycles to bad decisions when the validator is absent.

response.title: Five mandatory gates. AND-combined. No fast-loop bypass.
response.body: Mr. Mwikila runs every decision through the @borjie/loop-quality-gates package before it ever reaches a tool that touches the world. The composite gate runs all five checks in parallel and AND-merges the verdicts — one failure stops the loop.

gates.name: Groundedness gate
gates.blurb: Every factual claim cites a real EvidenceItem with a resolvable URL or system-of-record reference.

gates.name: Calibration gate
gates.blurb: Claimed confidence matches observed historical accuracy. Over-confident labels are downgraded or rejected.

gates.name: Brand gate
gates.blurb: Mr. Mwikila stays in voice. Design tokens only. No forbidden persona drift, no raw hex literals.

gates.name: Authority gate
gates.blurb: The action's tier must be inside the loop's granted authority. T2-Critical mutations route to a human.

gates.name: Budget gate
gates.blurb: Remaining ε-budget (tokens, wall-clock, tool calls) stays non-negative after the proposed action.

diagram.kicker: The 5-layer loop · validator gate visible between Decide and Act
diagram.gapLabel: Where IEEE + Snyk said the validator is missing
diagram.flowLabel: Loop closes back to Observe — every cycle re-grounded
diagram.caption: Observe, Orient, and Decide remain at machine speed. The Quality Gates layer slows just enough to verify groundedness, calibration, brand, authority, and budget — then Act fires. Failures route back to learning, never to the world.

Tanzania-regional primary storage with secondary failover in a regulator-approved region. Encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM, in transit with TLS 1.3. Sub-processors and storage regions are documented in /legal/subprocessors.

sections.title: 1. What we collect
sections.body: Borjie collects your mining business data — PML/ML/SML licences, pit maps, drill-hole logs, off-take transactions, royalty returns, and your users' messages. We do not collect personal data on individual artisanal miners unless they upload it themselves or an owner directs us to.

sections.title: 2. Why
sections.body: We use this data to run your Master Brain, deliver morning briefings, prepare reports for Mining Commission and TRA, and propose gold-window hedges. We do not sell it. We do not use it for advertising.

sections.title: 3. Where it lives
sections.body: Your data is stored in a Tanzania-resident database (Dar es Salaam region, with Fly.io fra1 as DR backup). Audit chains are per-tenant — your tenant cannot read another tenant's chain even if you are an admin.

sections.title: 4. Your rights
sections.body: Under the Tanzania Personal Data Protection Act 2022 you have the right to view, correct, delete, and port your data to another vendor. Email privacy@borjie.co.tz.

sections.title: 5. Mining Commission and NEMC
sections.body: If a Tanzanian authority (Mining Commission, NEMC, TRA, FIU) lawfully demands your data we will provide it, but we will notify you within 5 days unless a gag order is in place.

sections.title: 6. Contact
sections.body: This policy is updated periodically. Material changes get a 30-day in-cockpit notice before they take effect. Questions: privacy@borjie.co.tz.

sections.title: 1. Agreement
sections.body: By using Borjie you accept these terms. Borjie is Borjie Tanzania Limited, a Tanzania-registered company (BRELA registration: pending). The agreement is governed by Tanzanian law. Jurisdiction is the High Court of Tanzania, Dar es Salaam.

sections.title: 2. Tiers and billing
sections.body: Five tiers: Mwanzo (free), Mkulima (TZS 150,000/mo), Mfanyabiashara (TZS 500,000/mo), Kampuni (TZS 1,500,000/mo), Group (bespoke). Billed monthly in advance. Non-payment for 14 days pauses autonomous actions (but never the audit chain — your history remains yours forever).

sections.title: 3. Your data is yours
sections.body: You own your mining data. We hold it on your behalf. If you close the account we will export (or delete) your data within 30 days. The audit chain remains immutable per Mining Commission record-retention rules.

sections.title: 4. Master Brain and liability
sections.body: The Master Brain executes actions on your authority. We do not warrant flawless decisions, but we warrant that every action is recorded on the audit chain. If Borjie directly causes losses through a software defect, our liability is capped at the trailing 12 months of fees.

sections.title: 5. Prohibited use
sections.body: You may not use Borjie to conceal illegal activity, evade TRA royalties, or launder funds. If we detect suspicious activity we will report it to the relevant authority as required by law.

sections.title: 6. Termination
sections.body: You or we may terminate with 30 days' notice. On termination we export your data within 60 days. We may suspend immediately for material breach.

sections.title: 7. Contact
sections.body: Terms updates: 30-day in-cockpit notice. Questions: legal@borjie.co.tz.

about.heading: An operating system built for and by Tanzanian mining.
about.sub: Borjie was started by a small Tanzanian and diaspora team after watching another generation of operators run world-class deposits on spreadsheets.
about.originBody: We met across Geita and Dar es Salaam in 2024, swapping notes on why every mining ERP felt designed for someone else's country. By early 2026 we had a working Master Brain and ten pilots. The mission stayed the same: an operating system that respects how Tanzanian mining actually works — KYC for artisanal miners, royalty maths the TRA recognises, briefings in Swahili at 06:00.
about.teamSub: Tanzanian and diaspora. Mining, fintech, AI infrastructure. Hybrid Dar es Salaam.

values.title: Tanzanian-first
values.body: We optimise for the mine, the language, the regulator, and the currency our operators actually use.

pml.problemHeading: Why most PML sites lose money
pml.problemSub: Artisanal mining is rich in metal and poor in records. The leaks are not in the pit, they are in the paperwork.

problems.title: Royalty compliance gaps
problems.desc: Most PML holders miss filing windows because no one is tracking Mining Commission deadlines in the same place as production. Penalties stack quietly.

problems.title: Cash-in-hand payroll
problems.desc: Wages paid in cash leave no audit trail. When a licence audit lands, nobody can prove who worked which shift, who got paid what.

problems.title: Unverified buyers
problems.desc: Off-takers walk on site, weigh, pay below spot, and disappear. Without a verified marketplace, your gold leaves the country at a discount.

problems.title: Lost institutional memory
problems.desc: When a site manager leaves, the production history walks with them. New hires start from zero. No drill logs, no chain of custody, no records.

solutions.title: One brief, one truth
solutions.desc: Mr. Mwikila pulls production, royalty exposure, payroll status, and licence calendar into a single 06:00 brief on your phone. Bilingual sw and en.

solutions.title: Mobile-first chain of custody
solutions.desc: Every weigh-slip is photographed, voice-tagged, GPS-pinned, and timestamped. The audit trail is yours, immutable, and Mining Commission-ready.

solutions.title: Verified marketplace
solutions.desc: List your dore or gemstone parcels in a marketplace where KYB-cleared buyers compete on price. Settlement via mobile money or bank.

solutions.title: Built for the tablet
solutions.desc: The site manager runs it on one tablet. The owner reads it on the phone. The accountant exports it to TRA. One system, three roles.

pml.ctaHeading: See it on your site in 90 days.
pml.ctaSub: One pilot. No card. No commitment. Borjie reads your last 90 days of weigh-slips and shows you the brief running on your own data.

cooperatives.problemSub: Federated artisanal groups are the engine of Tanzanian mining. The tooling has not kept up with the responsibility.

problems.title: Manual member registers
problems.desc: Paper ledgers and Excel sheets. When a member queries their share, nobody can prove what they contributed, when, or in what condition.

problems.title: Bulk advances, unfair splits
problems.desc: Off-takers advance against pooled output. Members suspect (often correctly) that the split favours the well-connected.

problems.title: Inspection surprises
problems.desc: TCDC, Mining Commission, and TRA each want different cuts of the same data. Cooperatives spend weeks assembling instead of operating.

problems.title: Federated, but not connected
problems.desc: Each member runs their own pit. The cooperative aggregates by phone calls. Nobody sees the federation in one place, in real time.

solutions.title: Member-aware ledger
solutions.desc: Every delivery tagged to a named member, time-stamped, location-pinned, photo-attached. The audit trail is the cooperative's, not a notebook.

solutions.title: Transparent split
solutions.desc: Bulk advances flow into Borjie. Splits compute against tagged contributions. Each member sees their own ledger on their own phone.

solutions.title: Inspection-ready by default
solutions.desc: TCDC, Mining Commission, and TRA each get the slice they need, generated from the same ledger. Inspections take hours, not weeks.

solutions.title: Federation cockpit
solutions.desc: The chair sees pooled output, member-level performance, advance exposure, and decision queue on one screen. Real-time federation.

problems.desc: Royalty assignment letters live in scanned PDFs, when off-takers pay, the bank is invisible to the flow.

problems.title: Late covenant breach detection.
problems.desc: Quarterly reporting means the bank learns of a slippage 60 days after it happened.

problems.title: No BOT-aligned export pack.
problems.desc: When the borrower ships and clears, the bank cannot match the export to the underlying loan.

solutions.title: Licence-verified collateral.

solutions.title: Royalty assignment on rails.

solutions.title: Daily covenant telemetry.

faqs.q: How do I onboard my mine?
faqs.a: Sign up for Mwanzo (free) or Mkulima. Upload your PML / ML certificate and a recent royalty receipt. Mr. Mwikila takes you through the first-week brief. For Mfanyabiashara and above, a Borjie engineer schedules an onboarding call within two business days.

faqs.q: Can I import my existing spreadsheets?
faqs.a: Yes. Production logs, payroll runs, treasury statements and licence calendars import via a guided CSV / XLSX flow. Master Brain validates as you go and flags any rows that need review before they enter the ledger.

faqs.q: What languages does the platform support?
faqs.a: Swahili and English. The user can switch per device, and Mr. Mwikila replies in whichever language you wrote in.
Tanzania-regional primary storage with regulator-approved failover. AES-256-GCM at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Full sub-processor list at /legal/subprocessors.

faqs.q: Who do I contact about a billing question?
faqs.a: billing@borjie.co.tz for invoice or payment questions. M-Pesa, Tigo Pesa, Airtel Money, Visa, Mastercard and TZS bank transfer all supported.

faqs.q: What if I need urgent help?
faqs.a: WhatsApp +255 757 070 070 during office hours (06:00 to 20:00 EAT, weekdays). For after-hours and weekend incidents on Kampuni and above, your dedicated success channel covers 24x7.

supportPage.ctaSub: Schedule a 15-minute office-hours slot. We open files together.

securityPage.heading: Borjie is engineered for regulator scrutiny.
securityPage.sub: A 5-layer security model, audit-hash provenance on every artefact, and Tanzania-regional residency. Built for BOT, Mining Commission and NEMC, and aligned with ISO 27001 controls.
securityPage.pillarsHeading: Four pillars of the Borjie security posture.

pillars.title: Encryption everywhere
pillars.body: AES-256-GCM at rest. TLS 1.3 in transit. Application-layer envelope encryption for high-sensitivity fields (NIDA, biometric templates, royalty receipts).

pillars.title: Access on a need-to-know basis
pillars.body: Row-level security forced on every tenant-scoped table. Four-eye and sovereign-policy gates on every high-risk action. Mwikila Auditor logs every override.

pillars.title: Immutable audit chain
pillars.body: Every regulatory artefact (licence, royalty, return, HSE report) is hash-chained and append-only. Tampering is detected and refused at write-time.

pillars.title: Tanzania-regional residency
pillars.body: Primary storage in Tanzania, regulator-approved failover. Sub-processors and storage regions documented at /legal/subprocessors.

securityPage.auditBody: Borjie writes a signed, hash-chained record for every action that touches the ledger, the licence calendar, the royalty pipeline or the HSE feed. The chain is append-only at the database layer, mirrored to cold storage hourly, and exposed read-only to the regulator on request.
securityPage.ctaSub: Bring your audit checklist. We answer every line, on paper, with cited controls.

subprocessorsPage.heading: Every vendor in the loop, on one list.
subprocessorsPage.sub: Borjie keeps a complete, dated list of the sub-processors that touch tenant data. Updates are announced 30 days before they take effect.
subprocessorsPage.notice: Notice of a new sub-processor lands at /legal/subprocessors at least 30 days before it touches your data. Object by email to dpo@borjie.co.tz.

cookiesPage.heading: Every cookie we set, in one table.
cookiesPage.sub: We use the smallest set of cookies needed to run the marketing site and the platform. No advertising cookies, no third-party trackers without consent.

rows.purpose: Block cross-site request forgery on forms.

cookiesPage.manageBody: Adjust your consent any time. Essential cookies cannot be disabled without breaking sign-in and form submissions.
